title: An English Chronicle of the Reigns of Richard II, Henry IV, Henry V, and Henry VI
summary: The repository artifact is a pinned copy of University of Michigan CME item ACV5981.0001.001, a 2006 Level-1 TEI encoding made by uncorrected OCR from John Silvester Davies's 1856 Camden Society edition. The searchable XML transcribes printed pages 1-118 and retains page-break links through page 228, but pages 119-228 have no OCR text. Davies printed the main anonymous Middle English prose chronicle on pages 1-110 from the manuscript now Bodleian Library MS. Lyell 34, part 3 (fragment leaves 144a-h and fols. 145v-214v); selected Richard Fox material from Woburn Abbey MS 181 on pages 111-118; and Latin supplements from the continuation of the Eulogium in British Library Cotton MS Galba E VII on pages 119-142. A later edition by William Marx uses National Library of Wales MS 21608D as its base and Lyell 34 for comparison, so it is a related modern edition rather than an ancestor of the CME text. A complete public scan of the 1856 print edition is cached. No complete public digital facsimile was found for any manuscript witness; the manifest records whole-codex request routes and maps the target portion whenever the catalog evidence permits.
review_notes: The cataloged object is Davies's edited compilation, not a single medieval work: it joins the anonymous Davies Chronicle, selected Richard Fox passages, Latin Eulogium supplements, Davies's notes, and an index.

Davies treated the main chronicle as one compiler's work. Modern scholarship distinguishes two continuations, covering 1377-1437 and 1440-1461; anonymous responsibility is therefore retained without inventing named authors.
NLW MS 21608D is a parallel and now-preferred witness for the modern Marx edition. It was not the source of Davies's text or of the U-M encoding.

The surviving Lyell codex contains the earlier Brut before the portion Davies printed. Any reproduction request should ask for the complete manuscript, then associate this work with fragment leaves 144a-h and fols. 145v-214v.

QUB's MS 59 finding aid identifies a microfilm of Lyell 34 as MS 59/160b, but the public description says individual reels may reproduce manuscripts in whole or in part; the reel's coverage remains unverified.
Negative digitization findings are date-bounded to 11 July 2026 and should not be treated as permanent statements about repository services.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for acv5981
entities.description: Repository XML supplied through the pinned CME submodule.

entities.label: University of Michigan CME item ACV5981.0001.001
entities.description: A 2006 TEI-in-Libraries Level-1 encoding created by uncorrected OCR from the 1856 Camden Society edition and linked to 244 600-dpi TIFF G4 page images. Searchable text is present for printed pages 1-118; the remainder is represented by page-break milestones without OCR transcription.
notes: The XML header explicitly says that no corrections or content editing were performed after OCR.
The 244 PB elements link the encoded item to images for the complete printed volume even where searchable text is absent.

entities.label: John Silvester Davies's An English Chronicle (1856)
entities.description: The immediate print source of the CME item. Davies prints the Lyell 34 continuation on pages 1-110, selected Richard Fox material on pages 111-118, Eulogium supplements on pages 119-142, notes on pages 143-212, and an index on pages 213-228.

bibliographic.citation: John Silvester Davies, ed., An English Chronicle of the Reigns of Richard II, Henry IV, Henry V, and Henry VI, Written Before the Year 1471; with an Appendix, Containing the 18th and 19th Years of Richard II, and the Parliament at Bury St. Edmund's, 25th Henry VI; and Supplementary Additions from the Cotton MS. Chronicle Called 'Eulogium' (London: Camden Society, 1856), Camden Society old series 64.
bibliographic.title: An English Chronicle of the Reigns of Richard II, Henry IV, Henry V, and Henry VI

entities.label: Internet Archive scan of Davies's An English Chronicle (1856)
entities.description: A complete public scan of the printed volume, accompanied by OCR text. It includes all preliminaries, medieval texts, Latin supplements, notes, and index omitted from the CME searchable text.
entities.reproduction_format: Complete PDF page-image scan and OCR text derivative

entities.label: Bodleian Library, MS. Lyell 34
entities.description: The complete parchment codex used by Davies for pages 1-110. Parts 1-2 contain the English Brut through Edward III. Part 3, written by two secretary hands in or soon after 1461, contains the continuation printed by Davies; early leaves of that continuation survive only as damaged fragments.

holding.notes: Acquired by the Bodleian through James P. R. Lyell's bequest. De la Mare's official catalog is the exact reviewed item description.

physical_description.extent: vii + 219 leaves (the catalog notes that the physical sequence is effectively 227 leaves because of inserted fragment leaves 144a-h)
notes: Target portion: part 3, fragment leaves 144a-h and fols. 145v-214v; fols. 215-219v are blank.
The codex's earlier Brut text is not part of Davies's printed pages 1-110 but should be included if a complete-manuscript facsimile can be obtained.
The continuation has lost or badly damaged leaves near its beginning.

entities.label: Queen's University Belfast MS 59/160b microfilm of Bodleian MS. Lyell 34
entities.description: A repository microfilm entry for Lyell 34. The finding aid warns that its manuscript microfilms may reproduce sources in whole or in part, so this reel cannot yet be described as a complete-codex facsimile.

holding.notes: Consultation and reproduction conditions should be confirmed with QUB Special Collections; exact reel coverage is unresolved.

entities.label: National Library of Wales, MS 21608D
entities.description: A fifteenth-century English Brut manuscript and the base witness for William Marx's modern edition. Its continuation to 1450 includes, for 1415-1419, the same unusual text as the Davies Chronicle; a missing quire and a half probably once continued the text to 1461. It is a parallel witness, not a source used by Davies.

holding.notes: The catalog marks the original restricted and directs researchers to surrogate copies.

physical_description.format: Manuscript codex in original tawed-skin binding
notes: The catalog localizes the scribal dialect probably to the West Midlands or possibly Wales; this witness-level statement is not automatically assigned to every textual layer.

entities.label: William Marx's An English Chronicle 1377-1461: A New Edition (2003)
entities.description: A modern edition based on NLW MS 21608D and collated against Bodleian MS. Lyell 34. It distinguishes two anonymous continuations and provides a later scholarly comparison point rather than an ancestor of the CME encoding.

bibliographic.citation: William Marx, ed., An English Chronicle 1377-1461: A New Edition, Medieval Chronicles 3 (Woodbridge: Boydell Press, 2003).
bibliographic.title: An English Chronicle 1377-1461: A New Edition

entities.label: Woburn Abbey, MS 181
entities.description: The unique witness of Richard Fox of St Albans's substantial prose chronicle, completed in 1448 and extended with later material. Davies's pages 111-118 select accounts of the eighteenth and nineteenth years of Richard II and the 1447 Bury Parliament and death of Humphrey, duke of Gloucester.

holding.notes: Privately held at Woburn Abbey; research and reproduction requests are handled by the archive.

notes: The exact folios corresponding to Davies pp. 111-118 were not established from the reviewed public sources; they belong to the later material following Fox's main chronicle.

entities.label: British Library, Cotton MS Galba E VII
entities.description: A fourteenth-century Latin manuscript containing the Eulogium Historiarum at fols. 18r-208v. Davies prints selected Latin supplementary additions from its continuation on pages 119-142 and identifies individual manuscript folios in the printed headings.

date_statements.label: catalog dating
date_statements.value: third quarter of the fourteenth century

holding.notes: The current catalog exposes no digitised content and permits physical-item requests subject to repository conditions.

notes: The manuscript suffered damage in the 1731 Cotton Library fire.

entities.label: Albinia de la Mare's Catalogue of the Collection of Medieval Manuscripts Bequeathed to the Bodleian Library, Oxford, by James P. R. Lyell (1971)
entities.description: The authoritative reviewed item description for MS. Lyell 34, including codicology, foliation, contents, date, hands, provenance, and the exact target portion.

bibliographic.citation: Albinia de la Mare, Catalogue of the Collection of Medieval Manuscripts Bequeathed to the Bodleian Library, Oxford, by James P. R. Lyell (Oxford: Clarendon Press, 1971), pp. 85-87.
bibliographic.title: Catalogue of the Collection of Medieval Manuscripts Bequeathed to the Bodleian Library, Oxford, by James P. R. Lyell

entities.label: Anna Probert, Richard Fox of St Albans: Compiler of a Chronicle of English History (2023)
entities.description: Open scholarly study of Fox and the unique Woburn Abbey MS 181, used to distinguish Fox's compilation from Davies's selection.

bibliographic.citation: Anna Probert, 'Richard Fox of St Albans: Compiler of a Chronicle of English History,' Medievalista 34 (2023), https://doi.org/10.4000/medievalista.6934.
bibliographic.title: Richard Fox of St Albans: Compiler of a Chronicle of English History

agents.name: Frederic Madden
notes: Supplied Davies with the Richard Fox extracts printed on pp. 111-118.

agents.name: William Dunn Macray
notes: Compared press sheets with the Lyell manuscript and supplied corrections.

subject.description: Checked-in repository XML at the pinned CME commit

assertion.notes: Identifiers, header, structure, and content align; the current U-M XML export was not separately checksummed.

subject.description: Searchable OCR for pp. 1-118 plus page-break/image markers through p. 228

scope.notes: The XML searchable text stops after the Fox appendix. Eulogium supplements, notes, and index are represented only through PB milestones.

subject.description: Complete PDF scan and OCR derivative

object.description: Part 3 of the complete Lyell codex

scope.notes: The earlier Brut in parts 1-2 is present in the same codex but intentionally excluded from Davies's edition because it follows the common Brut text.

subject.description: Appendix containing Richard II year 18, Richard II year 19, and the Parliament at Bury

object.description: Later sections of Richard Fox's chronicle

scope.notes: Frederic Madden supplied the extracts to Davies.

subject.description: Latin supplementary additions from the Eulogium continuation

object.description: Passages identified individually by Davies within Cotton Galba E VII
locators: individual source folios printed beside each supplement

scope.notes: This relation covers Davies's direct Latin supplements, not every indirect Eulogium borrowing already translated into the anonymous Middle English chronicle.

subject.description: Microfilm reel MS 59/160b; exact coverage not stated

object.description: Bodleian MS. Lyell 34, potentially in whole or in part

scope.notes: Do not infer complete-codex coverage until QUB confirms the reel contents.

subject.description: Modern edition of the two continuations

object.description: The corresponding continuation in NLW MS 21608D

scope.notes: This is a later editorial lineage and does not lead to ACV5981.

object.description: Richard Fox's unique manuscript witness and chronicle

access.access_method: Read the pinned CME submodule file.
Open the U-M item page or stable handle; page-image links are exposed by the item.
access.format: HTML text with linked page images
notes: The searchable transcription ends at printed p. 118; use the complete 1856 PDF for the omitted back matter.

access.access_method: Download the complete PDF and OCR derivative from the exact Internet Archive file URLs.
Download the complete catalog PDF from Oxford University Research Archive; consult pp. 85-87 for Lyell 34.

local_copies.label: Complete De la Mare Lyell catalogue

work_portion.label: Catalog entry for the complete Lyell 34 codex

access.access_method: Date-bounded exact-shelfmark search in Digital Bodleian.

local_copies.label: Digital Bodleian exact-shelfmark search response
notes: Saved only as evidence of the date-bounded interface query; absence of a result can change.

access.format: No public IIIF manifest or page-image set located
notes: No exact shelfmark result was returned through the reviewed search interface/API on this date.

access.access_method: Submit the mediated-copying form by email. Ask first for a complete-codex color PDF; if repository limits prevent that, request part 3 and preserve the folio mapping.

local_copies.label: Bodleian Imaging Services terms of use

access.contact: mediated.copying@bodleian.ox.ac.uk (confirm the current address on the form/service page before sending)
access.format: PDF scans; service page states £1 per scan, £10 minimum, subject to feasibility and current pricing
notes: Desired complete-manuscript coverage: all of MS. Lyell 34. Target work portion within that complete facsimile: fragment leaves 144a-h and fols. 145v-214v.
Repository handling limits may make a whole-codex order expensive or unavailable; obtain a quotation and permission terms before ordering.

access.access_method: Contact QUB Special Collections to confirm whether MS 59/160b covers the complete Lyell codex and whether a digital reproduction can be supplied.
notes: The finding-aid PDF returned HTTP 403 to automated download attempts, so no local copy is claimed.

access.access_method: Use the official catalog record and request authorization/surrogate access from NLW; the original is marked restricted.
access.format: Restricted original; surrogate copies referenced by catalog
notes: No public complete digital facsimile or IIIF manifest was found on the review date. If a surrogate is supplied, request the complete 200-folio codex and map the continuation separately.

access.access_method: Submit Woburn's research-enquiry and copies/reproduction-permission forms by email; request a complete-codex facsimile where feasible and ask the archive to identify the Davies extracts' folios.

local_copies.label: Woburn copies and reproduction permission form

access.format: Research response and reproduction by arrangement
notes: The form warns of an approximately three-month enquiry backlog and possible charges; confirm current terms directly.

access.access_method: Download the complete open-access article PDF.
Use the exact Archives and Manuscripts catalog record to request the physical item or enquire about complete-codex imaging.
access.format: Physical manuscript; no digitised content listed
notes: If imaging is possible, request the complete codex. Davies's supplements derive from selected passages within the Eulogium at fols. 18r-208v and cite individual folios in the printed edition.

access.access_method: Purchase from the publisher or consult a library copy.

rights.component: Current U-M public item and its historical source materials
rights.basis: The current U-M item page states that the materials are in the public domain and links a No Copyright - United States rights statement.
notes: This is a provider statement for the current item. The legacy XML header separately says that copyright or other restrictions may apply, so downstream users should review the live terms.

rights.component: 1856 edition text and medieval source texts
rights.basis: The edition was published in 1856; the U-M provider identifies its materials as public domain in the United States.

The scan reproduces an 1856 edition; the file includes Google usage guidelines that request noncommercial use, which are recorded as access-provider terms rather than a conclusion about underlying copyright.
notes: Review the live Internet Archive item and embedded Google notice before redistribution of the scan file itself.

rights.component: A reproduction newly supplied through Bodleian Imaging Services
rights.basis: The mediated-copying form and Imaging Services terms state that supplied scans of out-of-copyright material are licensed CC BY-NC 4.0 and require separate permission for commercial use.
notes: This records the provider's stated supply terms, not an independent legal conclusion that copyright subsists in a faithful reproduction.

rights.component: Any image or reproduction supplied by Woburn Abbey Archive
rights.basis: Woburn's request forms require the intended use to be declared and reproduction permission to be obtained; exact terms and fees are supplied case by case.
The official record directs researchers to restricted-access procedures and surrogate copies but does not state reusable publication terms on the reviewed page.

rights.component: William Marx's editorial text, apparatus, and modern scholarly matter
rights.basis: The scholarly edition was published in 2003 and is sold by its publisher; no open license was identified.
The OpenEdition publication supplies the article under a Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial 4.0 license.

editorial_practices.source_feature: A complete Lyell codex whose earlier parts reproduce the common English Brut
editorial_practices.representation: Davies begins with Richard II and prints only the distinctive continuation in part 3.
editorial_practices.description: Davies deliberately excludes the earlier Brut and selects the later continuation as the main chronicle.
editorial_practices.scope: Printed pp. 1-110 from Lyell 34 fragment leaves 144a-h and fols. 145v-214v

editorial_practices.source_feature: Lost or damaged Lyell leaves and the chronicle's extensive borrowing from the Eulogium
editorial_practices.representation: Davies supplies separate Latin Eulogium passages as supplementary additions rather than silently reconstructing the Middle English text.
editorial_practices.description: The supplement documents gaps and sources at the edition layer; it is not manuscript text from Lyell 34.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Richard Fox's approximately 180,000-word chronicle in Woburn Abbey MS 181
editorial_practices.representation: Only three short late-medieval historical extracts supplied by Frederic Madden are printed.
editorial_practices.description: The appendix is an excerpted selection, not a complete edition of Fox's chronicle.
William Dunn Macray compared the press sheets against the Lyell manuscript and communicated corrections to Davies.

editorial_practices.source_feature: The complete 1856 volume of xvi, 228 pages
editorial_practices.representation: Searchable uncorrected OCR for printed pp. 1-118; PB/image references but no searchable text for pp. 119-228.
editorial_practices.description: The encoding is not a full textual transcription of the source volume even though it preserves a page-image sequence.

evidence.summary: Supplies the UMDL identifier, bibliographic header, 2006 conversion metadata, uncorrected-OCR statement, page-image specification, 244 PB milestones, and encoded structure through printed p. 118.

evidence.summary: Primary edition evidence for title, contents, editor's source statements, manuscript history, inferred compilation date, selection, supplements, Madden's contribution, Macray's collation, and exact printed boundaries.
Landing page and exact derivative links for the complete 1856 edition scan and OCR text.

evidence.citation: The Medieval Review, review of William Marx, ed., An English Chronicle 1377-1461: A New Edition (2003).
evidence.summary: Confirms Davies's use of damaged Lyell 34, Marx's use of NLW 21608D as base, the two-continuation model, and Eulogium source relationships.

open_questions.question: Has a complete public digital facsimile or IIIF Presentation manifest for Bodleian MS. Lyell 34 become available?
next_steps: Repeat exact-shelfmark searches in Digital Bodleian and Bodleian Archives and Manuscripts.
Ask Bodleian Imaging Services whether complete-codex scanning is feasible and obtain a quotation.
notes: Desired local object is one complete-manuscript bundle, with the work mapped to fragment leaves 144a-h and fols. 145v-214v.

open_questions.question: Does QUB microfilm MS 59/160b reproduce all of Lyell 34 or only part of the codex?
next_steps: Ask QUB Special Collections for a reel-level contents statement and digitization options.

open_questions.question: Which exact folios of Woburn Abbey MS 181 correspond to Davies's printed appendix on pp. 111-118?
next_steps: Ask the Woburn archivist to identify the folios while requesting a complete-codex reproduction.
Consult a detailed modern description or Marx's apparatus if available.

open_questions.question: Can complete-codex digital surrogates be obtained for NLW MS 21608D, Woburn Abbey MS 181, or Cotton MS Galba E VII?
next_steps: Contact each holding repository using the recorded access route and request whole-manuscript coverage rather than isolated work folios.
Record any supplied files as single complete bundles and add work_portion mappings.

open_questions.question: What dates and places can be assigned independently to the two anonymous continuation layers without transferring witness-level dialect or date evidence to the abstract texts?
next_steps: Consult Marx's introduction and subsequent textual scholarship.
Keep coverage dates, composition dates, witness dates, and scribal localization separate.
